summary: Iteration 7 retest (backend only). Both HIGH bugs from iteration 7 are FIXED. BulkAssign with rate_per_pair now returns 200 and persists rate on each affected job. BulkAssign without rate_per_pair falls back to worker default rate (18). MaterialIn now accepts reorder_level via PATCH and the value is persisted; GET /api/materials includes the field; GET /api/inventory/alerts correctly fires when balance<reorder_level and returns [] when all reorder_level=0.
passed: POST /api/production/bulk-assign with {job_ids:[J1,J2], role:'upper', worker_id:Ajay, rate_per_pair:22} -> 200, response.rate_per_pair=22.0, both jobs persist assignments.upper.rate_per_pair=22.0
POST /api/production/bulk-assign WITHOUT rate_per_pair (role:'finishing') -> 200, response.rate_per_pair=18.0 (falls back to worker default rate), assignments.finishing.rate_per_pair=18.0 on jobs
PATCH /api/materials/{CNV-002} with reorder_level=50 -> 200, response includes reorder_level=50.0; subsequent GET /api/materials returns reorder_level=50.0
GET /api/inventory/alerts with CNV-001(reorder=100,balance=0) and CNV-002(reorder=50,balance=0) -> 200 with 2 entries containing code, name, balance, reorder_level, shortfall fields
action_items: Iteration 7 HIGH bugs are resolved. Backend ready for first finish. Frontend UI verification (Payroll KPI tiles, Inventory alert banner, Materials reorder input) still deferred — recommend full testing agent run a Playwright pass post first-finish.

Minor: PATCH /api/materials currently requires full payload (code/name/category/unit/rate) — not a partial update. Not a blocker but consider a separate partial-update model if frontend sends only changed fields.
